info.title: ScaleGrid Rest API Documentation
info.description:
This is the documentation for ScaleGrid's REST APIs. Our detailed guides for every feature can be found [here](https://help.scalegrid.io/docs)


New to ScaleGrid? [Try ScaleGrid for free](https://console.scalegrid.io/users/register)! 


[Learn more about our offerings](https://scalegrid.io)

tags.name: General
tags.description:
This introductory section covers general APIs, required for most ScaleGrid workflows. We will cover authentication and checking the status of an asynchronous operation (aka job). 


Notes: 


* Before running any APIs, you have to login to console.scalegrid.io by making the [login call](#operation/login)

* You can logout from the console by making the [logout call](#operation/logout)

* The API set always returns a JSON document as output, with an object named "error". Always check if error.code equals 'Success' to decide if the API call succeeded.

tags.name: Cloud Profiles
tags.description:
This section covers APIs to work with cloud profiles. [Learn more about cloud profiles](https://help.scalegrid.io/docs/mongodb-new-cluster-bring-your-own-cloud)


Creating a cloud profile would be the first step before deploying any cluster. We will cover creating, updating and deleting cloud profiles. After creating a cloud profile, refer to the create-cluster method for your chosen database type - [MongoDB®](#tag/ScaleGrid-For-MongoDB(r)), [Redis™](#tag/ScaleGrid-For-Redistm), [MySQL®](#tag/ScaleGrid-For-MySQL(r)) or  [PostgreSQL®](#tag/ScaleGrid-For-PostgreSQL(r))

tags.name: Clusters
tags.description:
This section covers general cluster operations - e.g. patching the operating system. These APIs apply to clusters of all database types. 


Some of the most useful operations include


* Pause and Resume Clusters - if you have a test or analytics cluster that you do not require all the while, but also do not want to populate for each test, you can do pause/resume and save on your cloud hosting costs.
 
* Create a follower cluster - if you have a data analytics workflow running regularly, but do not want it to affect production, follower clusters can be very useful. [Learn more](https://scalegrid.io/blog/follower-clusters-3-major-use-cases-for-syncing-sql-and-nosql-deployments/)

* Monitor cluster health - forewarned is forearmed! Setup alerts on various metrics (e.g. percentage CPU and memory usage) to stay on top of your cluster. [Learn more](https://help.scalegrid.io/docs/mongodb-alerts-and-rules) 


To create a cluster first, refer to the create-cluster method for your chosen database type - [MongoDB®](#tag/ScaleGrid-For-MongoDB(r)), [Redis™](#tag/ScaleGrid-For-Redistm), [MySQL®](#tag/ScaleGrid-For-MySQL(r)) or  [PostgreSQL®](#tag/ScaleGrid-For-PostgreSQL(r))

tags.name: Projects & Maintenance Windows
tags.description:
This section covers Project specific and Maintenance-Window specific API calls. These APIs apply to clusters of all database types. 

[Learn more about Projects & Maintenance Windows](https://help.scalegrid.io/docs/projects-maintenance-windows)

tags.name: ScaleGrid For MongoDB®
tags.description:
This section covers MongoDB-specific API calls. The [Clusters](#tag/Clusters) section covers database agnostic calls that can be used on MongoDB clusters. [Create your own test environment to get started](https://console.scalegrid.io/users/register)


Before creating a cluster, you must find out the ID of the cloud profile(s) that'll be used to host your cluster. [Learn more about the different hosting strategies](https://scalegrid.io/blog/bring-your-own-cloud-byoc-vs-dedicated-hosting-at-scalegrid/)


* If you are using the [BYOC model](https://help.scalegrid.io/docs/getting-started-bring-your-own-cloud-plans), make sure to create a cloud profile on the cloud platform of your choice by calling the specific API in the [clouds section](#tag/cloudprofiles). Retrieve the id for this cloud profile from the [list clouds](#operation/listCloudProfiles) call. 

* If you are using the Dedicated Hosting Model, use the [list clouds](#operation/listCloudProfiles) operation to find a cloud profile in your preferred region

Using this cloud profile, you can now create a cluster of your choice, and automate a few workflows.

 A popular workflow among our customers is cloning your production cluster for development/QA. You can [create a standalone clone](#operation/peekAtBackup) from an [existing backup](#operation/listMongoBackups). Use [this](#operation/getMongoCredentials) API to obtain credentials of your newly created cluster, and you are set to go! Once the test is completed, you can [destroy the deployment](#operation/deleteMongoCluster).

post.description: Login to your ScaleGrid account. Login response contains cookies that need to be included in all subsequent calls.

username.description: The email address for your account

inputCode.description: Two Factor Authorization code if 2FA enabled

x-code-samples.source:
curl -k \
  https://console.scalegrid.io/login \
  -c cookiejar.txt \
  -d "{'username':'<Enter email address here>','password':'<Enter password here>', 'inputCode': '<Enter 2FA code here>'}"

schema.description: Cookies used to authenticate your API requests. You must specify all the cookies returned. If you are using cURL, please use `-c <filename>` when making the login and logout calls to save or remove the cookies to <filename>. Each subsequent call must use `-b <filename>` to send the cookies.

get.description: Logout of your ScaleGrid account to protect your data

parameters.name: PLAY_SESSION
parameters.description: Authentication cookie. Returned from the [login call](#operation/login).

get.description:
Check the status of an asynchronous operation. All asynchronous APIs return an unique actionID in their response, which must be used to monintor the job. 

 Notes -
 * A job is complete when progress = 100 and status != Running. 
 * A job is successful when status = Completed.

parameters.name: PLAY_SESSION
parameters.description: Authentication cookie. Returned from the [login call](#operation/login).

parameters.name: your-action-ID
parameters.description: ID of the job you wish to check. This is returned in the actionID field of the API call that starts a job

name.description: Name of the action being performed

object_name.description: Name of the object on which the action is being performed

object_type.description: Object type of the cluster. For example, MachinePool, MongoCluster, RedisCluster, MySQLCluster or PostgreSQLCluster

cancelled.description: Indicates whether or not the action has been cancelled. A cancelled action is never completed

progress.description: Value from 0 to 100 indicating progress of the action. If progress is 100 and status is Completed, the action is been completed successfully

status.description: Status of the action. When status in the response is 'Completed', it indicates the job completed successfully.

run_by.description: Email address of the user who began the action

post.summary: Create Script for Azure ARM Cloud Profile
post.description:
This API returns an Azure CLI or PowerShell Script to grant ScaleGrid the required permissions. On execution, this script creates a new ScaleGrid Resource Group and grants ScaleGrid permissions to it. Run this script before [creating an Azure ARM cloud profile](#operation/createARMCloudProfile). Be sure to use the same information is used in both API calls.

[Learn more about Azure ARM Cloud Profiles](https://help.scalegrid.io/docs/cloud-profile-azure-arm-setup)

[See what permissions ScaleGrid needs](https://help.scalegrid.io/docs/cloud-profile-azure-arm-setup#what-permissions-do-i-need-to-run-the-powershellazure-cli-script)

parameters.description: Authentication cookie. Returned from the [login call](#operation/login).

region.description: Region in which your database machines will be hosted

dbType.description: Database type that the cloud profile hosts

isPublic.description: If set to true, a static public IP will be created for every VM you create using this cloud profile.

useSingleTennantSP.description: Include to use single tennant service principal.

enablePremiumStorage.description: Include to enable premium storage for all VMs you create using this cloud profile.

get.description:
Returns an array of JSON objects describing all available cloud profiles. [Learn more about cloud profiles](https://help.scalegrid.io/docs/what-is-a-cloud-profile)


Use this API to locate an appropriate cloud profile to deploy your cluster on.

 * If you are using the BYOC model, use the `providerMachinePoolName` attribute to locate your cloud profile by name
 * If you are using the Dedicated Hosting model,

 ** First filter the response by `type` (i.e. the cloud on which you wish to host your deployment) and `dbType` (the type of database you wish to deploy)
 ** Then use the `regionDesc` field to find a cloud profile in your desired region.

Delete an old cloud profile. You can not delete a cloud profile if it is currently in use (i.e. at least one Running VM was created using this cloud profile)

Note: This is an asynchronous operation. [Check the job's status](#operation/getJobStatus) using the actionID

post.description: This API can be used to shut down the underlying VMs in your ScaleGrid Deployment. This would save you the infrastructure charges for your database, while it is not in use. You can use the [resume operation](#operation/resumeCluster) to bring your cluster up again Note: This is an asynchronous operation. [Check the job's status](#operation/getJobStatus) using the actionID. **WARNINGS**: * This action is only supported for BYOC Deployments. * This operation will shut down your database deployment * ScaleGrid Management charges will apply on all paused clusters.

parameters.name: PLAY_SESSION
parameters.description: Authentication cookie. Returned from the [login call](#operation/login).

clusterID.description: The ID of the target cluster. Access cluster IDs with the list clusters call.

dbType.description: Database type of the cluster to be patched

post.description:
Resume a paused cluster. This will bring up the underlying VMs, and then perform some health checks on the database to ensure consistency. Once your cluster is in Running state, you can start using it again!


Note: This is an asynchronous operation. [Check the job's status](#operation/getJobStatus) using the actionID.

parameters.name: PLAY_SESSION

dbType.description: Database type of the cluster to be patched

post.description:
Create a follower cluster that periodically syncs with another cluster. If a relationship already exists, you can use this API to change the import schedule.

[Learn more about follower cluster use-cases!](https://scalegrid.io/blog/follower-clusters-3-major-use-cases-for-syncing-sql-and-nosql-deployments/)

parameters.description: Authentication cookie. Returned from the [login call](#operation/login).

parameters.description: ID of the follower cluster. Access cluster IDs with the list clusters call

sourceClusterID.description: ID of cluster that will be followed

dbType.description: Database type of the target cluster

intervalInHours.description: Time in hours between each follower sync

startTimeStr.description: Start time of first sync. Format: ISO-8061

post.description: Syncs a follower cluster on-demand, from its source. This operation will overwrite all data on the target cluster with the latest backup from the source cluster.

post.description: Set the list of IP CIDRs that must be added to the firewall rules of a particular cluster. The list can be viewed using the [get firewall rules](#operation/getFirewallRules) call [Learn more about firewall rules](https://help.scalegrid.io/docs/account-firewall-rules) **WARNINGS**: * This action overwrites the existing IP CIDRs in your firewall rules. So in this call you must specify **all** the IPs you want to whitelist * This operation does not apply the firewall rules. Once the rules are set, [configure firewall rules](#operation/configureFirewallRules) to apply the rules to your cluster

post.description: Creates an alert rule. Returns a JSON object describing the alert rules for a particular cluster. [Learn more about alert rules](https://help.scalegrid.io/docs/redis-alerts-and-rules)

parameters.name: PLAY_SESSION
parameters.description: Authentication cookie. Returned from the [login call](#operation/login).

databaseType.description: Database type of the target cluster

operator.description: Paired with threshold to determine the condition when the alert will be generated - equal to, less than, greater than, less than or equal to, or greater than or equal to

averageType.description: Time for which the condition should persist before alert is triggered - two minutes, six minutes, hourly, or daily

The metric to setup an alert on. You can find the full list of metrics in our help docs -
 * [MongoDB](https://help.scalegrid.io/docs/mongodb-alerts-and-rules#mongodb-metrics)
 * [Redis™](https://help.scalegrid.io/docs/mongodb-alerts-and-rules#mongodb-metrics)
 * [MySQL](https://help.scalegrid.io/docs/mysql-alerts-rules#mysql-metrics)
 * [PostgreSQL](https://help.scalegrid.io/docs/postgresql-alerts-rules#postgresql--os-metrics)

averageType.description: Time before alert is triggered - two minutes, six minutes, hourly, or daily

clusterID.description: The ID of the target cluster. Access cluster IDs with the list clusters call.

created.description: Date the alert rule was created. Format: Unix timestamp. Time zone: UTC

post.description:
An asynchronous call to create a Dedicated or Bring Your Own Cloud (BYOC) Redis™ cluster. [Learn more about ScaleGrid for Redis™](https://help.scalegrid.io/docs/redis-new-cluster).


You can control the deployment type using the shardCount, serverCount and sentinelCount parameters.

 * For a three node master/slave deployment, use shardCount = 1, serverCount = 3, sentinelCount = 3
 * For a two node master/slave deployment, use shardCount = 1, serverCount = 2, sentinelCount = 3
 * For a three shard cluster mode deployment, with each shard having two nodes, use shardCount = 3, serverCount = 2


Note: This is an asynchronous operation. [Check the job's status](#operation/getJobStatus) using the actionID

parameters.name: PLAY_SESSION
parameters.description: Authentication cookie. Returned from the [login call](#operation/login).

sentinelCount.description: Number of sentinel nodes in the cluster.

serverCount.description: Number of nodes in each shard. 1 for standalone. For master/slave cluster, if this is an even number, ensure to set the sentinelCount as one higher than this.

encryptDisk.description: Set to true to enable LUKS-based disk encryption

clusterMode.description: Set to true to enable Redis™ Cluster Mode. [Learn More](https://redis.io/topics/cluster-spec)

cidrList.description: Only these IPs will be allowed access to the deployment. Mandatory if the specified cloud profiles are open to the Internet. For more information, click on this link.

shardCount.description: Number of shards in the cluster. 1 if clusterMode is false

machinePoolIDList.description: List of cloud profiles. Only 1 cloud profile for a Standalone cluster. The number of machine pool IDs should match the number of nodes in a replica set if it is odd and number of nodes in a replica set + 1 if it is even. Items in the list do not need to be unique.

backupIntervalInHours.description: Use this field to set up a Backup Schedule for your Redis™ deployment. Setting this to 0 disables backups.

parameters.name: PLAY_SESSION
parameters.description: Authentication cookie. Returned from the [login call](#operation/login).

shardCount.description: Number of shards in the cluster. 1 for Standalone or ReplicaSet

replicaCount.description: Number of data nodes in each shard. 1 for standalone. If this is 2, a quorum instance will automatically be added

machinePoolIDList.description:
List of cloud profiles IDs, one corresponding to each node to deploy. That is, for a standalone there should be only one value in the array, whereas for a replica set there must always be 3 values in the array.

Items in the list need not be unique - more than one node can be deployed using the same cloud profile.

Use the [list cloud profile](#operation/listCloudProfiles) to retrieve the ID of an existing cloud profile, or [create a new cloud profile](#tag/Cloud-Profiles)

engine.description: The storage engine to use - currently only InnoDB is supported

enableSSL.description: Set to true to enable SSL when connecting to your cluster

replicaConfig.description:
Use this parameter to control the type of replication to use.

* 0 - No replication (used for standalone clusters)
* 1 - Semi-Sync Replication
* 2 - Async Replication.

encryptDisk.description: Set to true to encrypt the data on the disk

cidrList.description: Only these IPs will be allowed access to the deployment. Mandatory if the specified cloud profiles are open to the Internet.

curl -k \

parameters.name: PLAY_SESSION
parameters.description: Authentication cookie. Returned from the [login call](#operation/login).

clusterName.description: Unique name for your cluster. Only alphanumeric characters are allowed.

version.description: The PostgreSQL version. Format: "V<major-version><minor-version>." [List currently supported versions](#operation/getDatabaseActiveVersions)

replicationType.description: The type of replication - asynchronous or synchronous. This parameter should be empty/null when creating a standalone cluster.

syncCommitType.description:
Specifies whether transaction commit will wait for WAL records to be written to disk before the command returns a 'success' indication to the client. [Learn more](https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/runtime-config-wal.html#RUNTIME-CONFIG-WAL-SETTINGS)

Notes -
 * If replicationType = ASYNC, syncCommitType can be either LOCAL or OFF
 * If replicationType = SYNC, syncCommitType can be either ON or REMOTE_WRITE or REMOTE_APPLY
 * We do not recommend setting syncCommitType to OFF. This can cause loss of committed transactions in the event of a server crash.

shardCount.description: The number of shards to deploy. Currently this is limited to 1

enablePgBouncer.description:
Set to true to enable connection-pooling via PgBouncer. [Learn more](https://help.scalegrid.io/docs/managing-pgbouncer)

Note: This setting can be changed after cluster creation using the [Enable PgBouncer](#operation/pgenablePgBouncer) or [Disable PgBouncer](#operation/pgdisablePgBouncer) APIs.

Use the [list cloud profile](#operation/listCloudProfiles) to retrieve the ID of an existing cloud profile, or [create a new cloud profile](#tag/Cloud-Profiles)

post.description:
Set the backup schedule for your cluster. Your cluster will regularly and automatically backup according to the schedule you set.[Learn more](https://help.scalegrid.io/docs/postgresql-backups-scheduled)

 Notes - 

 * When a cluster is created, a daily backup schedule (i.e. 24 hr interval) is set up by default.
 * You can use the [get backup schedule API](#operation/pggetBackupSchedule) to retrieve your current backup schedule
 * To disable scheduled backups call this API with scheduledBackupEnabled = false. You can omit all optional parameters

 * To enable scheduled backups, call this API with scheduledBackupEnabled = true. You **must** specify all the optional parameters

parameters.name: PLAY_SESSION
parameters.description: Authentication cookie. Returned from the [login call](#operation/login).

id.description: ID of your cluster. Access all cluster IDs with the [list clusters](#operation/pglist) call

scheduledBackupEnabled.description: Set to true to enable scheduled backups, and false to disable them.

backupHour.description:
Hour, in a 24-hour clock format, at which the first backup should be triggered. Subsequent backups are triggered in intervals of backupIntervalInHours. It can be any value between 0 and 23.

Note: All times are in UTC

backupIntervalInHours.description: Number of hours between scheduled backups.

backupScheduledBackupLimit.description: Limit on the number of scheduled backups to keep. Subsequent scheduled backups beyond this number will begin to replace the older scheduled backups.

target.description: The server which is used to back up the data. The target can be either 'MASTER' or 'STANDBY.' In a standalone cluster, the target should always be 'MASTER.'

pool_mode.description: This determines how soon connections return to the pool. [Learn more](https://www.pgbouncer.org/features.html)

pool_size.description: This determines the maximum number of cached connections per pool (i.e. per user + database combination). [Learn more](https://www.pgbouncer.org/config.html#default_pool_size)

max_client_connections.description: Maximum number of client connections (across all pools) that pgBouncer will allow. This can be higher than the max_connections set on PostgreSQL server. By default this is unlimited (i.e. value = 0) [Learn more](https://www.pgbouncer.org/config.html#max_client_conn)

max_db_connections.description: Maximum number of connections to a single database that pgBouncer will allow (across pools). By default this is unlimited (i.e. value = 0) [Learn more](https://www.pgbouncer.org/config.html#max_db_connections)

max_user_connections.description: Maximum number of connections by a single user that pgBouncer will allow (across pools). By default this is unlimited (i.e. value = 0) [Learn more](https://www.pgbouncer.org/config.html#max_user_connections)
